results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: Coordinated spam campaign with $1.5M options posts
Mixed signals between calls and puts narratives
results.topBullish: Exactly. Headlines move faster than reality. $META struggling to get enough compute isn't new and I doubt that changes overnight. Took the chance to buy the $IREN dip under $40, Let's see what Monday brings 👀
results.topBearish: Past 6 months: $IREN -20% SP500 +9% The reason for that 29% difference is instead of real deal and execution, just announce partnerships that are worthless. Greedy board: we deserved 18.2 million shares for not being down by 50%
results.summary: Sentiment on $IREN is severely distorted by a high-volume coordinated spam campaign posting near-identical '$1.5M+ options trade' messages referencing 34-strike contracts expiring 09/18, which are unreliable signals and likely artificial noise. Stripping out the spam, genuine organic sentiment is mildly negative, driven by significant underperformance versus the S&P 500, governance concerns around board compensation, and one explicit 'Avoid' recommendation — partially offset by opportunistic dip-buyers viewing the sub-$40 level as attractive.

results.name: Palantir
results.topBullish: @RnaudBertrand Palantir is the only company that is using AI in very practical real world situations IMO. They allow companies and organizations make leaps in execution quality and efficiency with AI. It's just not happening much in reality outside of them.
results.topBearish: 3 more countries are Firing Palantir: Spain, France, Germany. Way to go, fight the evil Palantir.
results.summary: Sentiment on Palantir is marginally bearish-to-neutral, with significant polarization between those viewing the company as a practical AI leader and critics citing surveillance overreach, government contract losses in Europe, and ethical concerns. Key drivers include ongoing debate over Palantir's AI/LLM architecture limitations, geopolitical pushback from European nations, NHS integration controversy in the UK, and spam/promotional activity inflating neutral post counts.

results.topBullish:
$KEEL: Wall Street Says Buy. The Chart Says Panic. Who's Right? 🤔

$Keel Infrastructure = the former Bitfarms ($BITF), rebranded April 2026 after redomiciling to the US and fully exiting Bitcoin mining for AI/HPC infrastructure.

📌 6 MONTHS OF ANALYST RATINGS (Fintel):
results.topBearish:
FYI $KEEL is about to close red in Canada 🇨🇦 after a 15% drop yesterday.
There is no rebound, the fall will continue next week
3$ coming very soon, then lower
results.summary: Sentiment on $KEEL is marginally positive but heavily diluted by a large volume of WhatsApp group spam posts that carry no genuine analytical signal. Among substantive posts, bulls cite the AI/HPC infrastructure rebrand and analyst buy ratings as catalysts, while bears point to a sharp 15% price drop and continued downside risk, creating a near-neutral overall outlook.

results.name: Affirm
keyThemes: spam and bot activity dominating feed
valuation debate: fintech vs credit business
results.topBullish:
Keep these on watch before July 8th.
• $COIN   — 165 → 285
• $HOOD  — 113→180
• $MSTR  — 101 → 165
• $SOFI  — 18 → 32
• $AFRM —85→ 130
• $PYPL  — 45 → 70
• $XYZ   — 79 → 120
• $NU  − 14 → 24
• $IBKR  — 91 → 135
• $CRCL  — 65 → 105
results.topBearish:
$AFRM has a strong brand, the merchant network is real, and the product actually sits inside checkout.

But the valuation question is brutal:

Is this a software-like payments platform?

Or a consumer credit business with cyclical losses, funding risk, and credit sensitivity?
results.summary: The overwhelming majority of posts about $AFRM in this sample are low-quality spam — repeated WhatsApp group recruitment messages and generic analyst target price links — which severely limits meaningful sentiment signal extraction. The few substantive posts present a mildly bullish price target narrative offset by a pointed valuation concern questioning whether Affirm deserves a premium multiple given its exposure to credit cyclicality and funding risk.

results.name: CrowdStrike
keyThemes: price target optimism and watchlist inclusion
results.topBearish:
Thinking of shorting $CRWD & $PANW NEXT 🤷🏻‍♂️

The reasons being that…
$CRWD trading at $195 per share & $200 B mcap and still -1050 PE meaning Net income is still negative growing 22-25% while trading at 40 P/S & split is done ✅
results.summary: Sentiment on $CRWD is mixed-to-slightly-bullish, with numerous posts placing it on watchlists and assigning aggressive upside price targets, while a meaningful cohort of bearish voices raises serious valuation concerns including negative GAAP earnings, a deeply negative P/E, and stretched price-to-sales multiples. The cybersecurity sector remains a favored thematic trade, but skeptics argue CRWD is overvalued relative to fundamentals and caution against adding at current levels.

results.name: Tesla
keyThemes: Delivery beat but stock sold off
results.topBullish: @SawyerMerritt @farzyness @Tesla Unsupervised deployment in Miami is the ultimate inflection point. Unlocking a major metro proves the vision stack clears regulatory hurdles. This transitions the fleet from driver-assist to a massive, high-margin revenue network. Huge step forward. $TSLA
results.topBearish: $TSLA's Semi just had its first fatal accident — a sobering moment as safety probes close. @Cordoba_trader will hammer this. @munster will argue one incident doesn't break the autonomy thesis. Reality check or overreaction? #Tesla #FSD
results.summary: Sentiment on $TSLA is cautiously bullish, driven by optimism around FSD autonomous deployment in Miami, Optimus production scaling, and a record Q2 delivery beat of 480K units that exceeded consensus by ~74K. However, bearish pressure persists from the stock's -7.5% decline post-earnings, valuation concerns at ~200x earnings, a fatal Semi accident raising safety scrutiny, and significant feed pollution from spam and promotional posts that dilutes signal quality.

results.name: iShares Bitcoin ETF
keyThemes: Bitcoin ETF outflows and selling pressure
results.topBullish: Similar price action with bullish divergence on the weekly! Relative strength to end the week. This seems interesting here. If you are looking for something longer term $IBIT or #Bitcoin is a great add in the accounts now, in my opinion at the 60,000
results.topBearish: Around $5.22 Billion in Bitcoin Has Been Sold via BlackRock's $IBIT in the Past 2 Months. May 2026: -$1.41 Billion June 2026: -$3.55 Billion July 2026: -$259.82 Million ( Only 2 days of July )
results.summary: Sentiment around $IBIT is marginally positive but broadly neutral, with meaningful bearish pressure stemming from significant reported outflows exceeding $5.2 billion over two months and concerns about quantum computing threats to Bitcoin. Bullish signals are limited to a handful of genuine long-term accumulation calls, while the majority of posts are spam, promotional content, or ambiguous ticker mentions that dilute any clear directional conviction.

results.name: Applied Digital
results.topBullish: Applied Digital plans $3.6 Billion expansion in Alexandria Louisiana creating 200 new jobs.
results.topBearish: Infrastructure, part 1: $APLD , $CIFR - have existing sell signals ♦️
results.summary: Sentiment toward $APLD is broadly bullish, driven by enthusiasm around its $3.6 billion data center expansion and frequent inclusion on sector rotation and dip-buying watchlists. Community engagement is high with dedicated WhatsApp and Discord groups forming, though a notable portion of posts are low-signal promotional or bot-like content, and a sell signal flag introduces a modest bearish counterpoint.
